key.name: monitors
key.description: The monitors whose brightness should be adjusted.

key.name: builtin-monitor
key.description: The name of the built-in monitor.

key.name: clone-mouse
key.description: When enabled, the mouse cursor follows the brightness setting. When disabled, the mouse cursor always remains at full brightness. Controlling mouse cursor brightness can sometimes show the wrong cursor and introduce cursor lag, and may prevent the display from sleeping (the software cursor loop keeps the screen active). Disable it if you encounter cursor issues or if your computer stops sleeping. Note that when other Gnome Shell components clone the mouse (like the Zoom accessibility feature), this feature may not work correctly, or there may be multiple cursors shown on the screen.

key.name: brightness-up
key.description: Keyboard shortcut to increase the brightness by one step (5%). Set to an empty list to disable.

key.name: brightness-down
key.description: Keyboard shortcut to decrease the brightness by one step (5%). Set to an empty list to disable.

key.name: debug-curve
key.description: For experimentation only; no UI. When non-empty, replaces the dimming formula with a lookup table: comma-separated output values (0..1) for evenly spaced input values from 0 to 1, at least 2 samples. Edit interactively with tools/curve-editor.py. Set to the empty string to return to formula-based dimming.

key.name: shader-gamma
key.description: How dimming treats dark areas. At 1.0 the screen scales down uniformly like a hardware backlight (contrast preserved). At 4.0 dark areas are left untouched and only bright areas are toned down to the brightness level (lower contrast at strong dimming). Values in between blend the two.
